title: Implement path-decoupled stable_id counter mechanism (KP-/KT- monotonic allocator)
description: Replace v1.x path-derived stable_id (agents-meta.ts:93-101) with a path-decoupled monotonic counter. Counter state lives in agents.meta.json under `counters.{KP|KT}`. Allocator function increments the appropriate counter when a NEW knowledge entry is created; existing entries keep their id across `git mv` (the only legal mutation is layer flip in rc.3, which regenerates id with knowledge.layer_changed event — out of scope here, just structurally supported). Provides `allocateKnowledgeId(layer, type, meta)` and `loadCountersFromMeta(meta)` / `persistCountersToMeta(meta, counters)` helpers used by TASK-007 scan and (later) rc.2 fab_extract_knowledge. Path-derived deriveAgentsMetaStableId remains as fallback for AGENTS.md/bootstrap-only edge cases.

files.change: Add `allocateKnowledgeId(layer: KnowledgeLayer, type: KnowledgeType, currentCounters: Counters): { id: string, nextCounters: Counters }` — pure function that increments the appropriate counter (KP for personal, KT for team) and returns formatted id + new counters object. Keep `deriveAgentsMetaStableId` for non-knowledge files (AGENTS.md/bootstrap) but mark it explicitly as fallback path. Update `withDerivedAgentsMetaNodeDefaults` to NOT auto-derive stable_id when a node has explicit knowledge metadata (type/layer present) — declared id wins.

New service that wraps allocateKnowledgeId with persistence. Exposes: `class KnowledgeIdAllocator { constructor(metaPath); async allocate(layer, type): Promise<string>; async getCounters(): Promise<Counters>; }`. Reads agents.meta.json, allocates, persists atomically (write to .tmp then rename). Handles concurrency via single-writer assumption (fabric is CLI-driven, no concurrent processes). Used by TASK-007 scan to mint baseline ids.

When buildRuleMeta encounters a node with frontmatter id field (set by TASK-004 parser change), use that id as stable_id verbatim — DO NOT regenerate from path. When writeRuleMeta serializes agents.meta.json, include `counters` field (initialized to {KP:0, KT:0} if absent). Never decrement counter on file delete (monotonic property).

implementation: Read agents-meta.ts:79-112 (withDerivedAgentsMetaNodeDefaults + deriveAgentsMetaStableId + deriveAgentsMetaIdentitySource) to understand current derivation flow
Add allocateKnowledgeId pure function in agents-meta.ts (no I/O)
Modify withDerivedAgentsMetaNodeDefaults: if node.stable_id is /^K[PT]-/ regex match → identity_source='declared'; skip path derivation
Modify rule-meta-builder.ts buildRuleMeta to honor declared knowledge ids
Modify rule-meta-builder.ts writeRuleMeta to persist counters envelope
Test: simulate `git mv .fabric/knowledge/decisions/foo.md .fabric/knowledge/guidelines/foo.md` (which would change v1.x derived id) — verify stable_id stays the same in regenerated meta
Test: counter monotonicity — allocate 3 KP-D ids, delete file with KP-D-2, allocate again → new id is KP-D-4 (NOT KP-D-2 reused)

reference.pattern: Existing identity_source 'declared' vs 'derived' bifurcation at agents-meta.ts:103-112; atomic file write pattern from doctor.ts (write .tmp then rename)
reference.examples: Reuse the 'declared identity_source' branch — knowledge entries are 'declared' just like rules with explicit <!-- fab:rule-id --> markers. Counter persistence pattern mirrors agents.meta.json revision sha256 pattern.

criteria: allocateKnowledgeId({layer:'team', type:'decision'}, {KP:0,KT:5}) returns id='KT-D-6' and nextCounters={KP:0,KT:6}
Two sequential allocations of same (layer,type) produce monotonically increasing counters (KT-D-6 then KT-D-7)
git mv test: file with frontmatter id='KP-G-3' moved between subdirs → buildRuleMeta produces stable_id='KP-G-3' (NOT regenerated from new path)
Counter never resets or decrements across delete-then-allocate sequence (KP-D-2 deleted → next allocation produces KP-D-4 if counter was at 3)
agents.meta.json after rc.1 init contains `counters: {KP: 0, KT: N}` where N = number of team baseline entries from scan
withDerivedAgentsMetaNodeDefaults returns identity_source='declared' for any stable_id matching /^K[PT]-/
Existing v1.x test fixtures (without counters field) still load via agentsMetaSchema with default counters {KP:0, KT:0}

rationale.chosen_approach: Pure allocator function + thin persistence wrapper; counters in existing agents.meta.json envelope
alternatives_considered: Separate counters.json file (rejected — adds another durable artifact + sync risk)
UUID-based ids (rejected — not human-readable per handoff key_findings)
Hash-based ids (rejected — not monotonic; collisions possible)
rationale.tradeoffs: Counter envelope means agents.meta.json is the single point of truth; corruption/concurrency risk exists but acceptable per single-writer CLI assumption

manual_checks: Inspect agents.meta.json after multiple init runs — counters strictly increasing
Move file between knowledge type subdirs — meta stable_id unchanged
success_metrics: 100% of git mv scenarios preserve stable_id
0 counter regressions across 50-iteration allocation stress test
